MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; Timothy D.F. Barteau, Esq. of Vasquez & Estrada LLP 453-0555 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. There are triable issues of fact based on the Mingram Declaration (plaintiff filed an executed original of the Declaration). Defendant's objection to the declaration is overruled. The Court cannot conclude that the failure to disclose Mingram in response to the special interrogatories was willfully done. Defendant's other criticisms of the Mingram declaration go to its ultimate persuasiveness, not to the question as to whether there is a triable issue of fact. The Court has not relied on the Ay Declaration in finding a triable issue of fact. Order signed in open court. Defendant's oral request for continuance was denied. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE LELAND STANFORD JUNIOR UNIVERSITY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: Paul Vaillancourt, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE FAILS TO MEET BAHL BECAUSE COUNSEL HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE ANY FACTUAL BASIS THAT SUGGESTS DOCUMENTS OR EVIDENCE EXIST TO OPPOSE THE MOTION. THE DECLARATION IS SILENT AS TO PLANT INSULATION DISCOVERY TAKEN IN THIS OR OTHER CASES, AND THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FILED NUMEROUS SUITS AGAINST PLANT INSULATION. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF CAN PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE ON PLAINTIFF'S BEHALF AS TO WHAT WORK DECEDENT PERFORMED AT STANFORD GIVEN THAT NO CO WORKERS WILL TESTIFY AND DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE ADMISSISBLE AS TO DEFENDANT.
